title: hieracium chondrillifolium subsp. comolepium
intro: Hieracium chondrillifolium subsp. comolepium, also known as hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its yellow daisy-like flowers and jagged leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Hieracium chondrillifolium subsp. comolepium include the potential relief of pain and inflammation, as well as its use as a digestive aid and antioxidant. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water.
